steps.title: Spot the contrast
steps.content: The contrast word while sets up opposition between traditional literary forms (like the short story) that remain recognizable, and rarer intergeneric forms that blur boundaries and become unclassifiable. The blank must describe forms with clear, stable identity.

steps.title: Apply the contrast to the choices
steps.content: Orthodox describes established, traditional forms that maintain clear identity—exactly the stable quality implied by recognizable. A, B, and C each miss the contrast: neutral doesn't address form classification, while equivocal and intermediate both suggest ambiguity rather than clarity. That matches choice D — D is correct.
